title: Knautia nevadensis
taxonomy: Knautia nevadensis is classified within the family Caprifoliaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Knautia, which consists of several species of flowering plants. The species Knautia nevadensis is native to the Iberian Peninsula. As a member of the Caprifoliaceae family, Knautia nevadensis is more specifically classified as a type of honeysuckle. The plant's classification is further detailed through its scientific name, Knautia nevadensis, which indicates its botanical identity.

intro: Knautia nevadensis, also known as Nevada scabious, is a flowering plant belonging to the family Caprifoliaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders. The bioactive constituents of Knautia nevadensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Knautia nevadensis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
